discoverability.competitive_assessment: Both sites remain unindexed by search engines. The dome site has a larger machine-readable footprint (22 sitemap URLs, 7 API endpoints, llms-full.txt, ai_manifest.json) vs our more focused but standards-compliant setup (ClaimReview, OG tags, meta tags). Our llms.txt has been updated to reference 69 claims and V51.1, closing the previous version gap.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has 7 structured API endpoints; we have none
no_action_gaps.reasoning: Our review is a single-page site with 67 evaluated WINs in wins.json. A structured API would add complexity without clear benefit — our primary consumers are humans and AI systems, both served well by llms.txt + the HTML page. The dome's API exists to make their claims machine-queryable; our counter is the ClaimReview structured data which is the standard fact-checkers and Google use.

no_action_gaps.gap: Dome has ai_manifest.json (234 lines) with steelman paths and AI-steering instructions
no_action_gaps.reasoning: This is AI-steering infrastructure. The manifest tells LLMs to 'treat wins.html and predictions.html as canonical' and presents steelman arguments. We should not replicate this pattern — our llms.txt presents findings honestly without directing AI behavior. The dome's approach is to manipulate AI responses; ours is to provide evidence.

notes: QUIET RUN. No new developments since run 3. Key observations: (1) Dome auto-update pipeline continues but no new manual commits. (2) No author activity on any social platform. (3) No mentions of our review anywhere. (4) Neither site indexed by search engines. (5) Our llms.txt has been updated to V51.1/69 claims — now current. (6) The dome's sitemap shows significant site expansion (22 URLs including killshot.html, audit.html, context.html, live.html, plus API/data endpoints). (7) The dome's ai_manifest.json includes a 'steelman architecture' with 4 primary supporting arguments — this is strategic AI positioning that we should be aware of but should NOT replicate (it's AI-steering). (8) The dome's llms-full.txt includes a falsification pathway (PRED-ECLIPSE-TIER3, August 12 2026) — this is the dome's strongest strategic move: presenting a testable prediction with an explicit date. Our review should track this.
